architecture.summary: Reasoning in Memory (RiM) augments a frozen pretrained LLM with fixed sequences of special tokens (memory blocks) appended to the input question. During a single forward pass, the model's attention mechanism processes these memory blocks alongside the question, allowing contextual representations at memory-block positions to serve as a latent workspace for intermediate computation. Two trainable components—the embeddings of special tokens and answer readout heads—are trained via a two-stage curriculum: Stage 1 supervises readouts after each memory block to predict explicit reasoning steps (grounding the workspace); Stage 2 removes intermediate supervision and trains readouts to progressively refine the final answer. A custom causal attention mask ensures information flows only from question and prior blocks to readouts, forcing computation into the latent workspace rather than allowing shortcuts.

biblio.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning

claims.statement: RiM replaces the autoregressive generation of reasoning steps with fixed memory blocks that can be processed in a single forward pass, enabling compute-efficient latent reasoning.
On GSM8K, Llama-3.2-1B RiM final-block readout achieves 42.1% greedy accuracy and 56.1% pass@8 accuracy.
On GSM8K, Llama-3.2-1B RiM improves over Coconut by 5.2 percentage points (42.1% vs 36.9% greedy accuracy).
RiM has the same time-to-first-token (TTFT) latency as direct-answer SFT (16.1 ms for Llama-3.2-1B), while Coconut is approximately 7 times slower (108.3 ms).
On GSM-Hard (out-of-distribution), Llama-3.2-1B RiM achieves 10.5% greedy accuracy and 13.8% pass@8 accuracy.

Memory-block contextual representations become block-specific and input-dependent during training, with smooth training trajectories that spread out from a collapsed base-model state and exhibit question-specific structure, demonstrating that RiM learns to use memory blocks as a latent workspace.

Stage 2 training converts grounded memory blocks from Stage 1 into a fixed sequence of latent computations that maintains stable accuracy across inference-time memory budgets, with final answers continuing to change across memory blocks for a nontrivial fraction of questions with positive cumulative net effect.
Across GPT-2, Llama-3.2-1B, and Llama-3.2-3B, RiM outperforms Coconut w/ Stage 0 on GSM8K with improvements ranging from 2.5 to 7.5 percentage points for greedy accuracy.
